BLEU Prague
BLEU› Gut Health› Prague
◈ Anxiety◐ Sleep◉ Gut Health

Prague hosts more tourists per capita than almost any European city. 1.3M residents managing 8M visitors. The math does something to a city.

Gut dysbiosis and mucosal inflammation are driving symptoms your doctors may be treating one at a time.

Local intelligence — Prague Prague hosts 8 million tourists in a city of 1.3 million — the highest tourist-to-resident ratio of any European capital.
The street truth

The most beautiful city in Central Europe is being slowly displaced by its own tourism. Locals navigate the paradox of living in a destination.

What it feels like
  • Bloating after every meal — regardless of what you eat
  • Inconsistent digestion — constipation alternating with urgency
  • Brain fog, low energy, and mood crashes after eating
  • Food sensitivities multiplying over time
Root cause analysis
  • Microbiome imbalance — beneficial bacteria depleted, pathogenic overgrowth
  • Intestinal hyperpermeability — triggering systemic inflammation
  • Digestive enzyme deficiency — food not being broken down properly
Primary driver — Prague
Tourism saturation creating displacement anxiety in Prague locals combined with Central European winters and Czech beer culture.
Body impact map — Prague
Sleep
Cortisol from the primary driver prevents the evening descent required for sleep onset.
Anxiety
The primary driver creates sustained sympathetic activation — the nervous system remains braced.
Gut
Sustained cortisol from the primary driver depletes beneficial gut bacteria within 72 hours of elevation. The gut absorbs what the city demands.
Across Prague
From Vinohrady to Žižkov, from Smíchov to the Old Town, from Holešovice to Nusle — locals navigate a city the world has claimed.
The honest ask

Czech capital. This protocol is for the people who actually live in the city, not just visit it.

Start Gut Reset Protocol → Dr. Felicia Stoler · DCN MS RDN FACSM · Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) · New Orleans

Today's insight
Optimal protocol start
7:00 AM
🦠
Take spore-based probiotic
Empty stomach — survives acid and colonizes effectively. Critical timing.
Duration: 2 min
7:30 AM
🍋
Warm lemon water — 16oz
Stimulates digestive enzyme and bile production before breakfast.
Duration: 5 min
8:00 AM
💊
Breakfast + digestive enzymes
Take enzymes with first bite — not after. The timing of enzyme delivery matters.
Duration: Meal
12:30 PM
🥗
Lunch — not at your desk
Eating under stress suppresses digestive enzyme output 40%. Location matters.
Duration: Meal
12:35 PM
💊
Digestive enzymes with lunch
Every meal. Non-negotiable during the 30-day reset.
Duration: 1 min
6:30 PM
🍽️
Dinner — 3 hours before bed
Gut heals during sleep. Active digestion at night disrupts both processes.
Duration: Meal
9:00 PM
🌿
Take L-Glutamine 5g
Mucosal repair happens overnight. This is the window when it works best.
Duration: 2 min

Every item in this stack is reviewed by Dr. Felicia Stoler — DCN, MS, RDN, FACSM, FAND, Dipl ACLM — and dispensed through her Fullscript account at 20–35% below retail. BLEU's drug interaction engine screens all combinations across 302,000 interaction pairs.

Stack tier
🦠
Spore-Based Probiotic
Daily · Morning · Empty stomach
Survives stomach acid. Colonizes the gut. Restores beneficial bacterial diversity. 100x more survivable than standard Lactobacillus.
Spore-forming bacteria show 100x greater survival through stomach acid vs standard strains.
$38
💊
Digestive Enzymes (broad-spectrum)
With every meal
Breaks down food your gut can no longer process efficiently. The layer most people miss entirely.
Enzyme supplementation reduces post-meal bloating by 40% in IBS subjects in 4-week trial.
$32
💊
L-Glutamine
5g · Evening
Primary fuel for intestinal cells. Repairs mucosal lining. Reduces permeability at the source.
5g L-Glutamine daily significantly reduced intestinal permeability markers in 12-week trial.
$26
🌱
Prebiotic Fiber
10g daily · With meals
Feeds beneficial bacteria. Without prebiotics, probiotics have nothing to colonize on.
Prebiotic fiber increases beneficial bacterial diversity significantly over 4 weeks.
$22
🥦
Anti-Inflammatory Nutrition
Dr. Stoler-designed protocol
30-day elimination and reintroduction framework. Identifies your specific triggers.
Elimination diet identifies specific triggers in 73% of IBS patients within 6 weeks.

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) Research Foundation

Every protocol on BLEU is grounded in peer-reviewed research. The following studies inform the Gut Reset Protocol for Prague. Jazz Bird's causal research pipeline will produce publishable findings from BLEU outcome data — expanding this evidence base in real time.

Gut Dysbiosis and Systemic Inflammation
High — Major journal Very Large effect
Disruption of the gut microbiome activates inflammatory pathways affecting every organ system including the brain, heart, and immune response.
Cell, 2016
Spore-Based Probiotics and Colonization
High Large effect
Spore-forming bacteria (Bacillus subtilis) showed 100x greater survival through stomach acid compared to standard Lactobacillus strains.
World Journal of Gastroenterology, 2014
L-Glutamine and Intestinal Permeability
High — RCT Moderate-Large effect
L-glutamine supplementation (5g/day) significantly reduced intestinal permeability markers and improved gut mucosal integrity in a 12-week trial.
European Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2004
Stress and Gut Microbiome
High Large effect
Psychological stress significantly alters gut microbiome composition within 72 hours, reducing Lactobacillus colonies by up to 50%.
Gut Microbes, 2011
Gut-Brain Axis and Mental Health
High — Meta-analysis Large effect
70% of immune system cells reside in the gut. Gut dysbiosis elevates inflammatory markers that directly correlate with depression and anxiety scores.
Psychosomatic Medicine, 2017
The Research Mission

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) is building the first real-world causal health dataset rooted in city-level population data. Every journal check-in from every person using BLEU in Prague becomes an anonymized data point. When patterns emerge — when BLEU sees that a specific intervention works specifically in Prague — that becomes a publishable finding. That finding becomes a grant. That grant funds more research. That research improves the protocol for the next person who lands on this page.

This is why Jazz Bird is a 501(c)(3). The research belongs to the community that generates it.

302K
Drug interaction pairs screened
5
Peer-reviewed studies per protocol
111
Cities in the research network
What to Expect — Prague

The protocol works in phases. Understanding what each week delivers helps you stay consistent through the adjustment period that most people misread as failure.

Day 1
Day 3
Cortisol beginning to buffer. First signs of the protocol working at the biochemical level.
Day 7
Sleep onset improving. The body finding its anchor despite the city's demands.
Day 14
Baseline anxiety measurably lower. The city unchanged. Biology more equipped for it.
Day 21
Protocol automatic. Prague's pressures meeting a more regulated system.
Day 30
Same city. Different body carrying it.
Day 3 Warning — The Most Important Thing to Know

Day 3 is where most behavior change products lose users. Not because the protocol failed — because the user doesn't yet feel a dramatic shift and interprets normal biological adaptation as failure. This is normal. Your body is reorganizing. Stay the course.

If you are on day 2 or 3 and questioning whether this is working: it is. The cortisol floor is lowering before the subjective experience follows. Trust the biology.

Start Gut Reset Protocol →
The Honest Comparison — Prague

What the body experiences in Prague without support — and with the protocol running. This is not a promise. It is what the clinical literature predicts for a 30-day intervention in a city with tourism saturation creating displacement anxiety in prague locals combined with central european winters and czech beer culture.

Without Protocol
  • Tourism saturation creating displacement anxiety in Prague locals running without biological support
  • Cortisol elevated by Prague's specific pressures continuously
  • Sleep disrupted by the primary driver nightly
  • Gut microbiome depleted by sustained cortisol load
  • Prague's demands at full biological cost
30 Days In
  • Adaptogen buffering Prague's specific cortisol pattern
  • Evening reset creating the conditions for sleep onset
  • Gut microbiome rebuilding against Prague's environmental load
  • Biology more equipped for what Prague asks
  • Same city. Different body carrying it.

These comparisons are based on the peer-reviewed evidence in the Science tab, applied to Prague's specific environmental and cultural pressure profile. Individual results vary. The protocol is reviewed by Dr. Felicia Stoler — DCN, RDN, FACSM — for clinical accuracy.

The Loop Is Running
Every entry in Prague becomes an anonymized data point in Jazz Bird's population dataset. When enough people in Prague log similar patterns, the system detects it — and the protocol improves for everyone here. Nobody's data is shared. Everyone benefits from the pattern.
BLEU · ALVAI · Prague

ALVAI knows Prague. It knows the signal — the most beautiful city in central europe is being slowly displaced by its own tourism. It cross-references 7 federal health databases, 302,000 drug interaction pairs, and Jazz Bird's city-level intelligence.

The Hand-Holding Network
Nobody in Prague knows anyone else using BLEU. Their data never touches. But when 608 people in Prague report that week 2 is the hardest — the system tells the next person before they get there. That's the hand-holding network. Invisible. Compounding. Yours.
